prompt:
Generate a continuous 400x400 coastal fishing map. North is land (~32%), south is open sea (~68%). From west to east, three fishing zones join as one natural coastline: beach (~36%), breakwater (~34%), rockycape (~30%). Do not place buildings, piers, harbors as towns, bridges, ships, lighthouses, caves, floating islands, or large offshore islands.

Overall: irregular but readable coastline with sandy cove, deeper central inlet, and rocky east cape. Highest land 12-18 blocks above sea level; most land 3-10. Seafloor deepest 12-16 below water; open ocean mostly 9-14; no trenches or submarine canyons.

Beach (west): casting beach as a gently curved cove, shore length 130-160, depth inland 20-55 (wider mid-cove, tapering ends). Layered wet sand / beach / dry sand / backshore dunes 2-5 high with gaps to grassland. Shore slope gentle; nearshore depth 1-3 (0-20 from shore), 3-6 (20-45), 6-10 farther. Sandy seafloor with sparse gravel/clay/small rock; keep casting water open and obstacle-light. Transition to breakwater via gravel/cobble pocket beach and 2-4 high rock knolls over ~20-40 blocks.

Breakwater (center): naturalized low stone mole (not architecture), length 90-130 seaward, width 5-8, crest 2-4 above water, slightly curved. Materials mixed stone/cobble/andesite/stone brick/mossy cobble with stairs/slabs/walls for uneven crest. Inner basin 70-100 wide: shore depth 3-5, center 7-11 bowl with a 1-3 deeper central trough; outer side 8-14. Quiet deep water inside for fishing; seagrass near roots only. Landward flat 4-7 above water as natural backshore only—no paths as pavement, yards, or facilities.

Rockycape (east): rough rocky shore length 120-160 with jutting rocks 2-8, narrow coves 3-8, low reefs 1-3 above water, small nearshore stacks diameter 2-10 (max ~15), thin channels, and shallow tide pools. Local cliffs 6-10 high and short; high points 12-18 above sea. Mix stone/cobble/andesite/tuff/deepslate/mossy cobble/gravel; darker rock near water. Complex nearshore: depths 2-5 shore, 4-8 among rocks, 8-14 offshore; small ledges, grooves, pockets, kelp/seagrass patches—no closed caves or long tunnels.

Backshore north: elongated grassland depth 40-100, gentle 1-3 steps and small hills, grass/dirt/coarse dirt/podzol/gravel/sparse stone. Open views to coast; trees 10-18% of backshore as small oak/spruce clumps (fewer on rockycape). One natural dirt/gravel path 2-4 wide linking beach rear → breakwater inlet → rockycape landward, gently weaving toward and away from shore without bridges or stairs.

Impression: west bright open casting beach; center sheltered deep mole basin; east rough rock fishing with stacks and channels. One continuous natural fishing coast.
